propositions: Release publication is gated only by a tag ref check with no verification that the tagged SHA is an ancestor of reviewed main, allowing an arbitrary pushed v* tag to publish.
Replace the v*-tag trigger with a workflow_dispatch-from-main trigger so the release source is reviewed-by-construction, enforcing github.ref == refs/heads/main.
The three version values across pyproject.toml, server.json top-level, and server.json packages are kept in lockstep only by a manual runbook, risking disagreeing published versions.
Add a pre-build version-lockstep check asserting version_input equals project.version and all server.json versions, failing before build on mismatch.
Add an ancestry assertion via git merge-base --is-ancestor with fetch-depth 0 as defense-in-depth against non-main sources.
Build and publish directly via PyPA Trusted Publishing OIDC (no tag relay, no PAT), creating the v* tag and GitHub Release only after successful publish as a record-only output.
